property_scores.justification: Precise alignment with education/nonprofit sector; strong multi-source evidence (ACNC, ACFID, tagline) with explicit confidence marking.
Highly specific niche definition (K-12 + vocational + holistic integration in Sub-Saharan Africa); rich evidence with concrete program examples.
Clear dual role as direct service provider (owns schools) and philanthropic intermediary; evidence of owned infrastructure and Australian fundraising operations.
Well-reasoned assessment of mature NGO sector with disruption signals; sector context research cited though forecasting future maturity inherently speculative.
Precise donation-dependent model with specific event (Gala Ball), corporate partnerships, and giving circles; explicit confirmation of no earned revenue.
Clear human-delivered, place-based service with specific school count (10), student numbers (4500+), staffing (250), and geographic details.
Fixed-cost-heavy identified with key drivers (staff wages, land, facilities); confidence moderate because detailed annual cost breakdown not available publicly.
Null profile_fill indicates no assessment provided; profile lacks evidence to evaluate network effects structural property.
Null profile_fill; no critical external dependencies assessment documented despite reliance on donor relationships and Australian HQ infrastructure.
Null profile_fill despite clear evidence of 250+ Ugandan + ~27-31 Australian staff available; property should be scored.
Estimated $2M-$5M AUD range based on Gala Ball and partnerships; confidence low due to lack of public financial statements.
Clearly identified as grant/donation-funded nonprofit with no equity/debt; cash reserves unknown, creating moderate coverage gap on financial fuel dimension.
Asset-medium to asset-heavy well-supported (43 acres, constructed buildings); Australian HQ asset status speculative (co-working vs leased).
Stable and growing trajectory evident from Gala Ball series growth and 17-year continuity; actual cash flow direction not disclosed.
Established (17-18 years) status confirmed by multiple evidence vectors: 2008 founding, blog post anniversary, consistent annual Gala Ball series.
Medium beneficiary base well-quantified (4500+ students, 250+ donors at Gala Ball); split between student/donor perspectives makes single score slightly generic.
Active growth clearly evidenced by 10 schools (vs 3 earlier) and Gala Ball scaling ($3M+ in 2025); growth rates not quantified.
Null profile_fill; not applicable as nonprofit without priced offerings, but segment should be documented as N/A with rationale.
Niche specialist positioning well-supported (locally-led model, holistic approach, founder credibility, event brand); no direct competitor comparison.
Relationship-led motion clearly documented with specific mechanisms (Gala Ball, Annabelle keynote circuit, corporate partnerships, content marketing).
Moderately concentrated with strong evidence of Gala Ball dominance and single-donor-family concentration risk ($1.5M match = 50% of 2025 event).
N/A for nonprofit; donor experience framed as premium but this is positioning, not pricing positioning; generic assessment limits coverage.
Founder-led (Annabelle) with co-founder transition inferred; board structure incomplete (RAWCS director confirmed, full composition unknown).
Hub-and-spoke topology clearly documented with specific team counts and geographic split; fine-grained reporting line details unavailable.
Multi-site distribution well-evidenced; Australian HQ modality (co-working vs dedicated) inferred from partial signals; Ugandan on-site status clear.
Mission-driven generalists + domain educators clearly characterized; career path example (Jess) provides strong signal; no quantitative distribution.
Minimal tech stack clearly documented (Squarespace, Google Classroom, GalaBid); no custom development with strong evidence.
Ad-hoc/no engineering function unambiguously confirmed by absence of tech roles and third-party service procurement model.
Minimal data infrastructure well-evidenced by tool inventory (Hello Bar, Google Classroom) and narrative impact reporting; no CRM/BI signals.
Minimal security posture appropriately assessed for nonprofit; no certifications or incidents; inherits third-party platform security; 17-year clean history.
Moderate dual-jurisdiction burden clearly documented (ACNC, ACFID, RAWCS conditions, Uganda NGO Bureau); compliance obligations well-specified.
Multi-continent operations precisely specified (Uganda field operations, Australian HQ, global communications); geographic footprint unambiguous.
Donor-cycle and forex sensitivities identified with reasonable evidence; major donor stickiness asserted but actual recession resilience unproven.
Nonprofit/charity structure precisely documented with specific obligations (ACNC filing, ACFID Code, RAWCS board condition, mission primacy).
Pragmatic posture clearly evidenced by selective tool adoption (Google Classroom) for proven need and 17-year model stability; high confidence.
Mission-driven positioning unambiguously evidenced: tagline, impact storytelling (named students), founder-as-face, premium event positioning.
Mission-driven values (locally-led, holistic welfare, gender equity) clearly constrain decisions with specific program examples (100 Women grant).
Strong-in-niche employer brand evident from talent retention (Jess decade-long career) and mission-aligned hiring signals; no Glassdoor data limits coverage.

coverage_scale.1.0: Precise match with specific evidence and multiple data points
coverage_scale.0.8: Clear with some gaps or generic elements

strengths: Industry & Vertical dimension: Perfect 1.0 (clear primary sector, niche, value chain, maturity)
Culture & Brand: Strong 0.91 (mission-driven positioning, founder brand, values clarity)
Operating Environment: Strong 0.89 (dual-jurisdiction clarity, regulatory obligations, geographic scope well-defined)

Highest individual scores: 1.0 across primary sector, niche, value chain, maturity, revenue/delivery models, company age, GTM, regulatory burden, geographic scope, governance, innovation posture, brand positioning, values, engineering practices
gaps: Scale & Resources: Weakest at 0.56 (revenue band is low-confidence estimate, headcount missing)
Market Position: 0.64 (customer segment missing, pricing position incomplete for nonprofit model)
Business Model: 0.73 (moat/network effects and key dependencies unfilled)
Technical Maturity: 0.68 (security posture uncertain, data infrastructure basic)
summary.data_quality: High-confidence research on mission, operations, financial trajectory, and governance. Lower confidence on financial specifics (annual burn, exact headcount, cash reserves) due to nonprofit information opacity.
